author: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon
book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.

metadata.author: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon

title: Canon XIII.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Strange and unknown clergymen without letters commendatory from their own Bishop, are absolutely prohibited from officiating in another city.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: No cleric shall be received to communion in another city without a letter commendatory.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: I have here followed the reading of the Greek commentators.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: But the translators of the Prisca, and Dionysius, and Isidore must have all read ἀναγνώστας (i.e., Readers) instead of ἀγνώστους.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Justellus, Hervetus, and Beveridge, as also Johnson and Hammond, follow the reading of the text.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Hefele suggests that if “Readers” is the correct reading perhaps it means, “all clergymen even readers.”
